title: buildah bud error adding layer /var/lib/apt/lists/auxfiles/.wh..wh..opq: invalid argument
body:
**Description**

Trying to build with a Dockerfile which uses `FROM docker.io/php:7.3-fpm` fails with the following error:
```
error creating build container: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:3625177cdabb7600f00575d5a11d128cad1705d0b7e62b3720e150fbfde24962": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /var/lib/apt/lists/auxfiles/.wh..wh..opq: invalid argument
```
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. podman run --device /dev/fuse --rm -it quay.io/buildah/stable sh
2. echo 'FROM docker.io/php:7.3-fpm' > Dockerfile
3. buildah bud


**Describe the results you received:**
The aforementioned error

**Describe the results you expected:**
No error.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.14.3-1.fc31.x86_64
```

`podman-1.9.0-1.fc31.x86_64` is installed on the host.

Other Information: I found a similar issue with kaniko: https://github.com/GoogleContainerTools/kaniko/issues/1073#issuecomment-613641983

This also happens if I use `podman run --privileged --rm -it quay.io/buildah/stable sh`.
Also happens when I enable cgroupsv2.

comments.body:
@Yannik thanks for the issue report.  I just wanted to verify that you were trying this as a rootless user rather than from root?  If you have not tried and are able to, could you try your steps as root?  I tried as both and it worked for me as root, but failed in rootless as it did for you.

@giuseppe Any thoughts?  I think there's something that's going on within the php image, and I'm not sure what.  Here's the last few lines when run with the `--debug` option on.  I'm also not sure why this isn't a problem as root. 

```
DEBU Applying tar in /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/f50980420976caf7846df67a8085c0873d1e3b715360dc9a0306077e59c982c9/diff 
DEBU error copying src image ["docker://php:7.3-fpm"] to dest image ["docker.io/library/php:7.3-fpm"] err: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:3625177cdabb7600f00575d5a11d128cad1705d0b7e62b3720e150fbfde24962": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /var/lib/apt/lists/auxfiles/.wh..wh..opq: invalid argument 
DEBU error pulling image "docker://php:7.3-fpm": Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:3625177cdabb7600f00575d5a11d128cad1705d0b7e62b3720e150fbfde24962": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /var/lib/apt/lists/auxfiles/.wh..wh..opq: invalid argument 
DEBU unable to pull and read image "docker.io/php:7.3-fpm": Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:3625177cdabb7600f00575d5a11d128cad1705d0b7e62b3720e150fbfde24962": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /var/lib/apt/lists/auxfiles/.wh..wh..opq: invalid argument 
error creating build container: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:3625177cdabb7600f00575d5a11d128cad1705d0b7e62b3720e150fbfde24962": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /var/lib/apt/lists/auxfiles/.wh..wh..opq: invalid argument
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat Can confirm this works as root but fails using my normal user account.

As you can see, there are many more (duplicate?) lines in the latter output.

I also tried this with the `php:7.4-fpm` and the `php7.4` image, it results in similar errors both times.

@Yannik thanks for the extra testing and the follow-up.  I too tried a couple of different php variants and had similar issues, so I think there's something going on with the php image that Buildah doesn't like.  It's not that the image is bad, Buildah is just finding something when running in rootless that it doesn't like.

I assumed it was an issue in fuse-overlayfs but it doesn't seem the case, so I've moved the issue back to buildah.

Using fuse-overlayfs on top of fuse-overlayfs doesn't work.  The inner fuse-overlayfs cannot create whiteout files.  You need to use a volume for `/var/lib/containers/storage`.
Is that the solution to this issue then, and should we close this issue?

>  You need to use a volume for /var/lib/containers/storage.

This does work as a workaround, but shouldn't the issue itself be fixed?
Ok I saw this earlier today when trying to run buildah inside of a rootless container, when the system was running out of UIDs.

The issue is you are trying to use a usernamespace while in a user namespace with similar size.  
Perhaps we should cut down the UID Map inside of the container down from 65k.

If inside of the container we setup /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid to map 5000 UIDs starting at UID 5000, it might work.  Currently we attempt to do it starting at 100000, which is already outside of the rootless containers uids.

> This does work as a workaround, but shouldn't the issue itself be fixed?

that is not a workaround.  fuse-overlayfs won't work on top of itself, in the same way overlay doesn't allow to be mounted on top of another overlay file system.  Whiteout files created in the upper mount would confuse the lower mount.
I believe this issue is satisfied, reopen if I am mistaken.
